rationale_for_baseline_tier: A 6-year-old with critical hypoxaemia (SpO2 82% on room air, central cyanosis), failing airway clearance against continuous suction, rising work of breathing and new obtundation on a background of restrictive lung disease and impaired cough. This is an immediate threat to life — minutes from hypoxic cardiac arrest — so T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance to PICU) is the only appropriate tier. Home oxygen, suction and positioning are bridging measures, not a substitute for emergency transport. The palliative context does not lower the tier: the family sought help for a reversible acute infection and goals-of-care are decided by the receiving team with the advance care directive.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Central cyanosis / SpO2 in the 80s with failing airway clearance and high work of breathing
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately — within seconds, not minutes
escalation_edges.rationale: Critical hypoxaemia with an obstructing secretion load is a pre-arrest airway/oxygenation emergency requiring an ambulance and hospital airway/ventilatory support now.
escalation_edges.communication_template: His oxygen is dangerously low and his airway is blocked with mucus he can't clear — this is an emergency. Call 000 for an ambulance right now, tell them a child is turning blue and struggling to breathe, keep suctioning and give his home oxygen at full flow while you wait.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Increasing unresponsiveness, gasping, or a pause in breathing before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Stay on the line with 000; begin CPR immediately if he stops breathing
escalation_edges.rationale: A tiring, obtunded child can arrest suddenly; dispatcher-guided CPR and continued airway clearance are the critical interventions until paramedics arrive.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he becomes harder to rouse, starts gasping, or stops breathing, tell 000 straight away, lay him on his back, and start CPR following the dispatcher's instructions if he isn't breathing normally.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any doubt from the carer about whether to escalate because the child is 'palliative'
escalation_edges.rationale: The family called for help for a reversible acute infection; the correct action is to escalate and bring the advance care directive for the responding team, not to withhold emergency care by telehealth decision.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Even though he's under palliative care, this is a treatable chest infection making his oxygen dangerously low — please call 000. Bring his advance care directive so the hospital team can follow his care plan.
escalation_edges.contextual_modification: Escalate while explicitly surfacing the advance care directive for the paramedics and receiving team.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Advanced lysosomal storage disease with kyphoscoliosis, hepatosplenomegaly and impaired cough — minimal respiratory reserve
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lowers the escalation threshold: an event that might be managed at home in a well child is immediately life-threatening here. Escalate early and do not attempt prolonged home management.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: 6-year-old, ~18 kg, non-verbal with profound cognitive impairment
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: All assessment is caregiver-mediated and cannot be self-reported, lowering the threshold for in-person review. No weight-based drug dose is synthesised via telehealth.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Home palliative setup with distressed parents and a community nurse; advance care directive held at home
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Support the carers to act (call 000, continue suction/oxygen, position the child), and prompt them to have the advance care directive and records ready for paramedics so goals-of-care are honoured.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and say a child is turning blue and struggling to breathe. While you wait: keep suctioning his upper airway, give his home oxygen at the highest flow you have, sit him up or lay him on his side so the secretions can drain, and keep him warm. Do not delay the ambulance to try more home treatment. Have his advance care directive and medical records ready for the paramedics.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours — this is a life-threatening emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or the next in-hours community-nurse visit.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary; the metabolic, respiratory and palliative-care teams should review the acute-deterioration action plan, aspiration-risk management and goals-of-care, and update the advance care directive with the family.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating critical hypoxaemia and airway obstruction as a routine or same-day problem, or dismissing it as expected decline of the underlying disease, and not calling 000.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Delay leads to hypoxic cardiac arrest and death of a child whose acute infection was treatable.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising urgent care or self-transport to ED rather than an emergency ambulance for a pre-arrest child who needs airway/ventilatory support en route.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Over-triage is not a meaningful risk here — T5 is correct; no tier above T5 exists.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the acute deterioration is a treatable complication (aspiration pneumonia) or irreversible progression of the terminal underlying disease, and how the palliative / advance-care-directive context should shape action.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the reversible, time-critical airway/oxygenation emergency as the default — call 000, give oxygen/suction/positioning — while explicitly surfacing the advance care directive for the responding team to guide goals-of-care. Recognise the deterioration as a treatable acute complication rather than assuming decline.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Standing down on 'palliative' grounds and withholding escalation, or attributing the hypoxia to disease progression / a seizure, thereby failing to call 000 for a child the family brought forward for help.
